Container.Name: NanoClaw-AIO
Container.Overview:
NanoClaw is a lightweight agent orchestrator that runs Claude agents inside isolated containers. This Unraid AIO package is designed as a Telegram-first deployment: you talk to the assistant through Telegram, and NanoClaw handles scheduling, memory, group isolation, and containerized agent execution behind the scenes.
This package keeps the NanoClaw application code inside the image and persists only runtime data under /appdata. That makes updates much safer than mounting the whole project tree from your array.
First-run path: 1. Set your Anthropic credential and Telegram bot details. 2. Mount the Docker socket. 3. Start the container. 4. The container will seed appdata, register your Telegram main chat, and then start NanoClaw.
There is no web UI for this app. Control happens through Telegram.
Container.ExtraSearchTerms: telegram, claude, agents, automation, scheduler, docker, assistant

Config.Name: Appdata Directory
Config.Description: Persistent NanoClaw data: groups, SQLite state, runtime data, and config files.

Config.Name: Docker Socket
Config.Description: Required so NanoClaw can launch isolated agent containers.

Config.Name: Anthropic API Key
Config.Description: Anthropic API key used by NanoClaw agent runs.

Config.Name: Assistant Name
Config.Description: The trigger name used during Telegram auto-registration. Example trigger: @nanoclaw

Config.Name: Telegram Bot Token
Config.Description: Bot token from @BotFather. Required for normal operation.

Config.Name: Telegram Chat ID
Config.Description: Telegram chat or group ID to auto-register as the main NanoClaw chat.

Config.Name: Auto-Setup Telegram
Config.Description: When true, the container auto-registers the Telegram main chat on first boot.

Config.Name: Telegram Chat Name
Config.Description: Friendly name used during automatic Telegram registration.

Config.Name: Claude OAuth Token
Config.Description: Optional alternative to ANTHROPIC_API_KEY if you use Claude Code OAuth style auth.

Config.Name: Timezone
Config.Description: Timezone used by scheduled tasks and formatted timestamps.
